idea_summary: SIMT FP32 FMA baseline kernel - async copy + named barrier pipeline
WGMMA+TMA kernel - CuTe DSL limitation: TMA shape mismatch, cpasync F16 alignmen
Larger K tile (16) and more pipeline stages (4) - improves memory latency hiding
Reduced MN tiles 64x64 - reduces register pressure and local memory spills
MN=64x64, K=32 - larger K tile reduced performance, possibly more register press
6 pipeline stages - slight regression, more stages increased SMEM pressure
128x64 tiles, 128 threads - better per-thread work distribution and L2 reuse
WGMMA F16 - CuTe DSL copy partition shape mismatch with swizzled SMEM layouts
64x128 tiles - worse M dimension is better for L2
128x64x32 with 5 stages - larger K hurts with 128 threads
8-element padding worse - original no padding is better
K=8 3 stages worse than K=16 4 stages
128-bit copy requires 128-bit aligned source - not available for row-major FP32
Unroll outer loop by 2 improves ILP
128x32 64 threads - illegal memory access (64 threads is incompatible)
1024 threads exceeds max threads per block
